L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where model files are loaded using an unsafe deserialization method. A security scanning mechanism is in place to detect malicious payloads, but it can be bypassed because scan errors are silently ignored rather than treated as failures. Additionally, scanning is disabled by default in certain code paths. An attacker can exploit this by embedding malicious code in a crafted model file and submitting it through the model installation API, achieving remote code execution.

L3.area: Model checkpoint loading, scanning, and probing utilities
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where model checkpoint files are loaded using a pickle-based deserialization method. A security scanning mechanism is in place to detect malicious payloads before loading, but it can be bypassed because scan errors, such as invalid file format errors, are silently ignored rather than treated as failures. Additionally, scanning is disabled by default when reading checkpoint metadata. An attacker can exploit this by embedding malicious code in a crafted model file and submitting it through the model installation API, achieving remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The model loading pipeline uses torch.load (pickle-based deserialization) to load model files, and the security scanner's error results are silently ignored, allowing crafted malicious model files to bypass scanning and execute arbitrary code upon loading.
